# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security

FROM docker:24-cli

RUN apk update && \
    apk upgrade && \
    apk add --no-cache \
        ca-certificates \
        tini \
        bash && \
    rm -rf /var/cache/apk/*

RUN addgroup -S orchestrator && \
    adduser -S -G orchestrator -h /app -s /bin/bash orchestrator

WORKDIR /app

COPY --chown=orchestrator:orchestrator . /app/

RUN chmod -R 750 /app

# The container needs access to the host Docker socket.
# Mount it at runtime: -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
# The orchestrator user needs to be in the docker group on the host.
# At runtime, pass the host's docker group GID:
#   --group-add <host-docker-gid>
# This avoids running the entire container as root.

ENV DOCKER_HOST=unix:///var/run/docker.sock

# Drop all capabilities by default; at runtime grant only what's needed:
#   --cap-drop=ALL --cap-add=NET_BIND_SERVICE
# Do NOT use --privileged in production.

# Health check to verify Docker socket connectivity
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD docker info > /dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1

# Read-only root filesystem recommended at runtime:
#   --read-only --tmpfs /tmp:rw,noexec,nosuid,size=64m

# Security options recommended at runtime:
#   --security-opt=no-new-privileges:true
#   --pids-limit=100
#   --memory=512m
#   --cpus=1

USER orchestrator

ENTRYPOINT ["/sbin/tini", "--"]

CMD ["bash"]
